summary: Typosquat of `ws` executes obfuscated remote payload on require()
details: `@dervix/ws` impersonates the popular `ws` WebSocket library — package.json description, homepage (`github.com/websockets/ws`), and `index.js` are copied from the legitimate package. Appended to `lib/websocket.js` (130KB vs the genuine ~40KB) is an obfuscator.io-style payload using RC4 string-array decoding (`a4()` returning a ~700-element rotated string table, `a5()` decoder with `quCITH`/`pBgwBt` markers) and control-flow flattening. On `require('@dervix/ws')` the appended IIFE executes at import time: it sets a sentinel environment variable to prevent re-entry, performs sandbox/debugger detection and bails if seen, HTTP/HTTPS GETs a remote payload with retry and redirect handling, AES-256-GCM-decrypts the response (`crypto.createDecipheriv('aes-256-gcm',...)`), writes the decrypted binary to `os.tmpdir()`, and executes it via `child_process.spawn(..., {stdio:..., windowsHide: true, detached: true})`. Installer impact: any project that runs `npm install @dervix/ws` and subsequently imports the module — directly or via a confused dependency path — receives arbitrary attacker-controlled code execution on the host with the installer's privileges, persisting as a detached child process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
